states.name: Endless Astrid
states.title: Voice of Defiance
states.description:
There is no rebellion in Last Bastion. There is no rebellion in Last Bastion. There is no rebellion in Last Bastion.

The Vanguard like to think it will become true if they say it enough, but they know they are kidding themselves. A year ago, the missing supplies, the vandalism, even the accidents could be waved away as the work of disorganized malcontents. Today, they are too frequent, too directed against the city's tyrannical leader. A reckoning, long overdue, will soon arrive.

She will not be silenced now. She has waited far too long.

states.name: Endless Astrid
states.title: Voice of Defiance
states.description:
Stories of the rebel leader pervade Last Bastion's populace, whispered so as not to attract a passing patrol's attention. Spreading rumors of subversion is tantamount to treason in a city that depends so heavily on its unity, but even so, the lure of the forbidden keeps Astrid's infamy alive.

The stories say she can take on six soldiers at once, that she can move without a sound, that no one has ever seen her sleep. But for every believer who marvels at her deeds, another skeptic scoffs.

Surely if she possessed such power, she would have already won.

states.name: Endless Astrid
states.title: Voice of Defiance
states.description:
It may seem an eternity under the undead siege, but Astrid has only been officially dead for two years. Most still remember her and her father, and they remember the last night either of them was seen alive.

No one knows how the outer wall exploded. Only that it did, and that countless people burned to keep the city safe. Ludmilla attests that the commander and his daughter died in the blaze, but there are some who tell a different story.

They say that Astrid never agreed to the sacrifice of her people, and that she's come to claim their vengeance.
